parentQuestion: A charged particle is generally called an ion or electrolyte.

scaffolds.explain: An ION is a CHARGED atom or molecule. Ions form when atoms gain or lose electrons, creating positive or negative charges.

wrongExplains.0: Neutral atoms have balanced charges; ions are charged.
wrongExplains.2: Ions are charged particles, not cells.
wrongExplains.3: Ions are charged particles, not organs.

scaffolds.q: What gives an atom its electrical charge?
scaffolds.explain: An IMBALANCE BETWEEN PROTONS AND ELECTRONS gives atoms their charge. More electrons = negative; fewer electrons = positive.

wrongExplains.0: Neutrons are neutral; proton/electron imbalance creates charge.
wrongExplains.2: Nucleus size doesn't determine charge; electron balance does.

options: A solid crystal that doesn't dissolve
A substance that dissociates into ions when dissolved
scaffolds.explain: An ELECTROLYTE is a substance that DISSOCIATES INTO IONS when dissolved in water. These ions can conduct electricity.

wrongExplains.0: Electrolytes dissolve and dissociate, unlike non-dissolving crystals.
wrongExplains.2: Fats are lipids; electrolytes are ion-forming substances.
wrongExplains.3: Proteins are macromolecules; electrolytes form ions.

scaffolds.q: What happens when table salt (NaCl) dissolves in water?
options: It separates into Na⁺ and Cl⁻ ions
scaffolds.explain: When NaCl dissolves, it SEPARATES INTO Na⁺ AND Cl⁻ IONS. This dissociation is why salt is an electrolyte.

wrongExplains.0: Dissolving means the crystal breaks apart into ions.
wrongExplains.2: Salt dissolves into ions in water, not into gas.
wrongExplains.3: Salt dissociates into existing ions, not new compounds.

scaffolds.q: Why are dissolved ions called electrolytes?
scaffolds.explain: Dissolved ions are called ELECTROLYTES because they can CONDUCT ELECTRICITY. Moving ions carry electrical charge.

wrongExplains.0: Electrolytes conduct electricity, not produce light.
wrongExplains.2: Electrolytes conduct electricity, not primarily generate heat.
wrongExplains.3: Electrolytes conduct electricity, not create magnetic fields.

scaffolds.q: What are common electrolytes in the body?
scaffolds.explain: Common body electrolytes include SODIUM, POTASSIUM, CALCIUM, CHLORIDE, and BICARBONATE. These ions regulate fluid balance and nerve function.

wrongExplains.0: Vitamins are organic molecules; electrolytes are ions.
wrongExplains.2: Fats are lipids; electrolytes are dissolved ions.
wrongExplains.3: Proteins are macromolecules; electrolytes are ions.

scaffolds.q: What is the difference between cations and anions?
options: Cations are positive; anions are negative
scaffolds.explain: CATIONS are POSITIVE (lost electrons); ANIONS are NEGATIVE (gained electrons). Both are types of ions.

wrongExplains.0: Cations and anions have opposite charges.
wrongExplains.2: Charge, not size, distinguishes cations from anions.
wrongExplains.3: Sodium is a cation, not an anion.

scaffolds.q: Why are electrolytes important for body function?
options: They enable nerve impulses, muscle contractions, and fluid balance
scaffolds.explain: Electrolytes enable NERVE IMPULSES, MUSCLE CONTRACTIONS, and FLUID BALANCE. Ion movement is essential for all these functions.

wrongExplains.0: Electrolytes don't provide calories; carbs, fats, proteins do.
wrongExplains.2: Electrolytes are essential for physiology, not just taste.
wrongExplains.3: Electrolytes affect many systems, not just digestion.

scaffolds.q: Is this TRUE: 'A charged particle is generally called an ion or electrolyte'?
options: False — ions and electrolytes are unrelated
True — ions are charged particles, and electrolytes dissociate into ions
scaffolds.explain: TRUE — IONS are charged particles, and ELECTROLYTES dissociate into ions when dissolved. Both terms relate to charged particles.

wrongExplains.0: Ions and electrolytes are closely related concepts.
wrongExplains.2: Both cations and anions are charged.
wrongExplains.3: Electrolytes produce charged ions when dissolved.
